assertion: Analysis 'instantiation-vs-reference-classification'. Supporting: decisive_move: "Third chapter of a four-part project in which each exhibition's own photographs and collages are fed back as training data for the next - literalizes AI model collapse as a visible, physically-installed material process, and draws an explicit parallel between data mining and geological mining extraction." Contradicting: decisive_move: "A physical 'quantum cyborg' sculpture (mild steel, fibreglass, acrylic, plus AR layers) staging Bosch's Garden of Earthly Delights as a warning about quantum computing's coming acceleration of AI-driven surveillance capitalism; Heaney explicitly frames dissolving/'sliming' as a strategy of resistance against detection systems." decisive_move: "An open, participatory lab inside a spatial-audio dome where invited artists collectively 'reopen the black box' of generative AI models in public, treating the training/generation process itself — not just its output — as the artistic material." decisive_move: "Group exhibition of GAN-trained films, a real-time AI dialogue-generating website, and an 'artificial counter-intelligence machine,' collectively probing where AI's competence gives way to abjection and epistemological breakdown." decisive_move: "Three chatbot models (ChatGPT, Claude, Mistral) are each given a character and voice through a speaker on one of three benches arranged in a triangle, and left to converse live about AI anxiety and 'the end of grand narratives' — an unscripted, looping machine dialogue restaged as improvised theatre, restarting every seven minutes." decisive_move: "Kurant feeds her own writings into GPT-2 and GPT-3 and renders the output as a holographic animation, literally closing the loop of a model trained on and regurgitating one author's own words — a small, self-contained demonstration of the recursive self-consumption/collapse dynamic at stake in cluster 7." decisive_move: "Dancers improvise alongside a generative-AI avatar to invent speculative alternate lives for a prehistoric Indus Valley bronze statue, using an AI model as collaborator in reanimating an artifact whose original meaning is otherwise irrecoverable." decisive_move: "An AI trained on real Biennial and Whitney archives endlessly hallucinates fake artist biographies and press releases, exposing algorithmic curation's absurdity by actually letting a machine attempt it, live, forever." decisive_move: "An AI video model is cast as a stage magician performing sleight-of-hand; rather than concealing the model's characteristic failures (hands multiplying, morphing, defying physics), the film keeps every glitch on screen as the actual 'trick' — generative failure turned into the evidentiary content." decisive_move: "Large-scale dome installation built from 6,500 digitized African pictographs, syllabaries and ideographs (extending the artist's 2019–2023 'Ancestral Algorithm' project) that reframes 'algorithm' as 'algo + rhythm', proposing indigenous knowledge systems as an alternative computational epistemics rather than an ornament to it." decisive_move: "Fifteen writers respond in fiction to Italo Calvino's 1967 essay on cybernetics and myth, paired with a companion album, using the generative-AI moment as a mirror held up to older mythic and oral world-building traditions." decisive_move: "A video installation braids Peruvian plant-healing practices together with 'quantum-inspired' technology, treating indeterminacy and non-Western epistemics of plant communication as equally valid registers of knowledge alongside computation." decisive_move: "An AI-driven virtual biome of post-human creatures listens to and choreographs itself from ambient audio feedback captured live in the gallery, proposing folklore-derived 'other' intelligences as an alternative to extractive machine learning." decisive_move: "First chapter of a planned 1,000-year, 32-generation project in which successive artists 'feed' one AI so it can eventually make a film witnessing humanity's end — a deliberately glacial experiment in what a machine 'learns' across centuries rather than scraped-in-seconds data." decisive_move: "Extends Cheng's neurosymbolic autonomous-agent architecture (originally built for BOB, 2018-19) into a live, indefinitely-running simulation of a pet turtle's daily belief-updating - an actually-executing inferential AI system exhibited as artwork rather than a fixed narrative or render."

conditions: work must be catalogued in the pinned works-atlas snapshot, not merely mentioned in secondary commentary
theory-side corroboration is drawn only from the pinned theory-atlas snapshot's own model-collapse keyword match over tags/summary/work
scope.population: works in the pinned works-atlas snapshot (corpora/model-collapse/works-atlas.snapshot.json, sha256:9d14e877efb245cc04b8451734b26285a274dda0418f5131db257d2e4312d373, 214 verified data/AI artworks) whose own `clusters` array contains 7 ("AI self-consumption" per src/data/field/clusters.json) — corroborated against the pinned theory-atlas snapshot (corpora/model-collapse/theory-atlas.snapshot.json, sha256:f712ea4e9c6b9137fa180ad91e73a86d8d09862792f33174c77acd76a891e610, 87 provenance-verified sources) for the model-collapse mechanism's own existence
scope.time: atlas snapshots pinned 2026-07-21 at the recorded content hashes
